skill.name: backend-agent
skill.description: Guides backend API, database, authentication, RLS, and integration work for the Unite-Hub Next.js and Supabase application.
skill.summary: Builds and reviews secure Unite-Hub backend routes, database access, authentication, and integrations.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples and scanner keyword collisions rather than executable malicious code. The skill is still medium risk because it directs agents to modify authentication, database isolation, third-party integrations, and AI prompt construction in production backend code.

medium_findings.title: High-impact backend modification guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to change authentication, API routes, RLS policies, and workspace filtering. This is legitimate backend guidance, but mistakes could expose tenant data or weaken access control.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly direct authentication and data-isolation changes. The risk is operational rather than malicious, so confidence is high for impact but not for hostile intent.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted CRM content is interpolated into an AI prompt
medium_findings.description: The Claude integration example inserts interaction history and contact data directly into a generation prompt. This can allow stored user content to influence generated output unless callers add prompt-injection controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt construction is clear, but this is an illustrative example and not executable skill code. A human should verify downstream safeguards before production use.

low_findings.title: Command and backtick detections are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The external command alerts come from fenced Markdown blocks and example shell or TypeScript snippets. I found no evidence that the skill executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is inside Markdown code fences and describes examples for an agent to implement. No runtime script, hook, or installer is present in the audited file.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access is configuration guidance
low_findings.description: The environment variable findings show standard Google and Anthropic SDK configuration. No evidence found of secret logging, credential exfiltration, or unauthorized network transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The variables are passed to SDK constructors as expected configuration. The skill does not include code that prints or sends those secrets elsewhere.

low_findings.title: Sensitive-file and weak-crypto alerts are keyword collisions
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM and weak cryptography alerts map to RLS SQL, prose, table indexes, or agent names. No evidence found of Windows credential access or weak hash implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary SQL or documentation context, not filesystem paths or cryptographic calls. The scanner matched substrings without semantic support.

content.value_statement: Backend changes can break authentication, tenant isolation, or integrations. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for secure Unite-Hub API and database work.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of Next.js API route handlers with validation and structured errors.
Applies Supabase workspace and organization filters to database queries.
Reviews authentication checks for browser and server API flows.
Defines RLS policy patterns for workspace-scoped Supabase tables.
Shows integration patterns for Gmail, Stripe, and Claude API clients.
Provides checklists for backend security, logging, and multi-workspace tests.
limitations: It is tailored to the Unite-Hub application structure and naming conventions.
It does not execute migrations, deploy code, or verify live Supabase policies by itself.
It includes example code that must be reviewed before production use.
It does not provide advanced rate limiting, GraphQL, webhooks, queues, or Redis caching.

use_cases.title: Secure a new API route
use_cases.description: Create a Unite-Hub endpoint with authentication, validation, workspace filtering, error handling, and audit logging.

use_cases.title: Review tenant data isolation
use_cases.description: Find database queries that need workspace or organization filters and align them with RLS policy expectations.

use_cases.title: Implement service integrations
use_cases.description: Add Gmail, Stripe, or Claude integration code while keeping credentials in environment variables and API routes protected.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic API Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-agent skill to create a Unite-Hub API endpoint for this feature. Include authentication, input validation, workspace filtering, and clear error responses.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Missing Workspace Filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-agent skill to review this route for missing workspace and organization filters. Explain each data-isolation change before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Add Supabase RLS Policies
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-agent skill to design RLS policies for this new Supabase table. Cover SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Integration Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-agent skill to review this Gmail, Stripe, or Claude integration. Check credential handling, authorization, audit logging, and failure behavior.

output_examples.input: Create a bulk contact update endpoint for one workspace.
output_examples.output: A route implementation plan with required fields, authentication checks, workspace-scoped updates, structured errors, and audit logging requirements.

output_examples.input: Audit this contact intelligence route for data isolation issues.
output_examples.output: A concise review that identifies missing workspace filters, explains tenant exposure risk, and lists exact changes to apply.

output_examples.input: Add RLS policies for a campaign enrollments table.
output_examples.output: A policy checklist covering enabled RLS, workspace membership checks, allowed actions, and tests with users from different organizations.

best_practices: Provide the relevant route, table schema, and expected authorization model before asking for changes.
Ask for workspace and organization isolation checks on every query that reads or mutates tenant data.
Review generated integration code for secret handling, logging behavior, and production error messages.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to create public endpoints without an explicit security exception.
Do not accept generated RLS policies without testing users from different workspaces.
Do not paste live API keys, OAuth tokens, or customer email data into prompts.

faq.question: What projects is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for Unite-Hub backend work using Next.js, Supabase, authentication, and third-party integrations.

faq.question: Can it create complete API routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide route structure, validation, authentication, database access, errors, and audit logging.

faq.question: Does it manage Supabase RLS policies?
faq.answer: It provides policy patterns and review guidance. You must apply and test policies in your Supabase environment.

faq.question: Does it need access to secrets?
faq.answer: No. It can reference environment variable names, but you should not provide real secret values in prompts.

faq.question: Is it safe for multi-tenant applications?
faq.answer: It emphasizes workspace and organization filtering, but generated changes still need code review and tenant isolation tests.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
